name: float_to_scaled
description: Converts a floating-point number to a scaled integer representation. This is used for precise storage of decimal values like material volumes, where floating point inaccuracies should be avoided. # Arguments * `val` - The floating-point value to scale. # Returns A tuple containing: 1. The significand as an i32 (the digits without the decimal point). 2. The precision as an i32 (the number of decimal places).

name: get_stock
description: Retrieves the stock record for a specific product. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `product_id` - The ID of the product. # Returns The stock record for the product.
signature: pub fn get_stock(key: String, product_id: i32) -> Result<Stock, String>

name: get_all_stocks
description: Retrieves all stock records from the database. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. # Returns A list of all stock records.
signature: pub fn get_all_stocks(key: String) -> Result<Vec<Stock>, String>

name: insert_stock
description: Inserts a new stock record for a product. Validates the quantity range and ensures the product exists. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `product_id` - The ID of the product. * `quantity` - The initial stock quantity. # Returns The newly created stock record.
signature: pub fn insert_stock(key: String, product_id: i32, quantity: i32) -> Result<Stock, String>

name: update_stock
description: Updates the quantity of an existing stock record. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `product_id` - The ID of the product. * `quantity` - The new stock quantity. # Returns The updated stock record.
signature: pub fn update_stock(key: String, product_id: i32, quantity: i32) -> Result<Stock, String>

name: remove_stock
description: Removes a stock record by its ID. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `stock_id` - The ID of the stock record to remove. # Returns The number of deleted records.
signature: pub fn remove_stock(key: String, stock_id: i32) -> Result<usize, String>

name: export_stock_data
description: Exports all stock data to a file (CSV, XLSX, or ODS). # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `path` - The destination file path. * `format` - The export format (csv, xlsx, or ods). # Returns An empty result on success.

name: import_stock_data
description: Imports stock data from a file (CSV, XLSX, or ODS). Updates existing stock records or inserts new ones. Emits progress events to the frontend during the import. # Arguments * `app` - The Tauri application handle for emitting events. * `key` - The database encryption key. * `path` - The source file path. * `format` - The import format (csv, xlsx, or ods). # Returns The number of imported records.

name: get_settings
description: Retrieves the current application settings. # Returns The current application settings object. #[command]

name: save_settings
description: Saves the provided application settings to disk. # Arguments * `settings` - The application settings to save. # Returns An empty result on success. #[command]
signature: pub fn save_settings(settings: AppSettings) -> Result<(), String>

name: get_storage_info
description: Retrieves information about the current application storage and data paths. # Returns Storage info including paths for database and images. #[command]

name: migrate_image_directory
description: Migrates the image storage directory to a new path. Moves all existing image files to the new location and updates their database paths. Restricted to subdirectories of the application's local data directory for security. # Arguments * `app` - The Tauri application handle. * `key` - The database encryption key. * `new_path` - The target directory for image storage. # Returns An empty result on success. #[command]

name: create_recipe_list
description: Creates a new recipe list for a specific product. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `product_id` - The ID of the product for which the recipe is being created. # Returns The newly created recipe list metadata.
signature: pub fn create_recipe_list(key: String, product_id: i32) -> Result<RecipeList, String>

name: get_recipe_list_by_product
description: Retrieves the recipe list associated with a specific product. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `product_id` - The ID of the product. # Returns An optional recipe list if one exists for the product.

name: delete_recipe_list
description: Deletes a recipe list by its ID. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `list_id` - The ID of the recipe list to delete. # Returns The number of deleted records.
signature: pub fn delete_recipe_list(key: String, list_id: i32) -> Result<usize, String>

name: add_recipe_item
description: Adds a new item (material) to a recipe list. Scales the volume use based on the provided precision. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `recipe_list_id` - The ID of the recipe list. * `material_id` - The ID of the material product being added. * `volume_use` - The amount of material used. * `unit` - The unit of measurement for the material. # Returns The newly added recipe item.

name: get_recipe_items
description: Retrieves all items belonging to a specific recipe list. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `recipe_list_id` - The ID of the recipe list. # Returns A list of all items in the specified recipe.
signature: pub fn get_recipe_items(key: String, recipe_list_id: i32) -> Result<Vec<RecipeItem>, String>

name: update_recipe_item
description: Updates an existing recipe item's volume use and unit. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `item_id` - The ID of the recipe item to update. * `volume_use` - The new amount of material used. * `unit` - The new unit of measurement. # Returns The updated recipe item.

name: delete_recipe_item
description: Deletes a recipe item by its ID. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `item_id` - The ID of the recipe item to delete. # Returns The number of deleted records.
signature: pub fn delete_recipe_item(key: String, item_id: i32) -> Result<usize, String>

name: create_invoice
description: Represents an accumulated report of products and materials sold within a date range. #[derive(Serialize)] pub struct AccumulatedReport { pub products: Vec<ProductAccumulation>, pub materials: Vec<MaterialAccumulation>, } Represents the total quantity of a specific product sold. #[derive(Serialize)] pub struct ProductAccumulation { pub product_id: i32, pub title: String, pub total_quantity: i64, } Represents the total volume of a specific material used in products sold. #[derive(Serialize)] pub struct MaterialAccumulation { pub material_id: i32, pub name: String, pub total_volume_used: i64, pub precision: i32, } Creates a new invoice (receipt header) for an optional customer. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `customer_id` - The ID of the customer associated with the invoice, if any. # Returns The newly created invoice header metadata.
signature: pub fn create_invoice(key: String, customer_id: Option<i32>) -> Result<ReceiptList, String>

name: add_invoice_items
description: Adds items to an existing invoice and updates stock levels accordingly. Supports both direct product stock and recipe-based material stock. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `receipt_id` - The ID of the invoice header. * `items` - A list of product IDs and their respective quantities. # Returns An empty result on success.

name: get_invoice_detail
description: Retrieves the header and all items for a specific invoice. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `receipt_id` - The ID of the invoice. # Returns A tuple containing the invoice header and a list of items in the invoice.

name: get_invoices_by_date
description: Retrieves all invoices created within a specific Unix timestamp range. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `start_unix` - The start of the date range in Unix seconds. * `end_unix` - The end of the date range in Unix seconds. # Returns A list of invoice headers within the specified date range.

name: get_accumulated_report
description: Generates an accumulated report of products and materials sold within a date range. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `start_unix` - The start of the date range in Unix seconds. * `end_unix` - The end of the date range in Unix seconds. # Returns An accumulated report containing summarized product sales and material usage.

name: get_products
description: Retrieves all products with their associated images from the database. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. # Returns A list of products with their associated image metadata.
signature: pub fn get_products(key: String) -> Result<Vec<ProductWithImage>, String>

name: create_product
description: Creates a new product with the specified details. Validates input length, price range, and ensures the product name is unique. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `title` - The name of the product. * `category_id` - The ID of the category this product belongs to. * `satang` - The price of the product in satang (1/100 of the currency unit). * `use_recipe` - Whether this product uses recipe-based stock tracking. # Returns The newly created product.

name: update_product
description: Updates an existing product's information. Performs validation on title length, price, and ensures name uniqueness. Also synchronizes the product price in the stock records. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `id` - The ID of the product to update. * `title` - The new name for the product. * `category_id` - The new category ID for the product. * `satang` - The new price for the product in satang. # Returns The updated product.

name: delete_product
description: Deletes a product by its ID. Before deletion, checks if the product is referenced in past receipts. Cleans up associated stock records and image links. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `id` - The ID of the product to delete. # Returns The number of deleted records.
signature: pub fn delete_product(key: String, id: i32) -> Result<usize, String>

name: set_product_stock_mode
description: Sets whether a product uses recipe-based stock tracking. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `id` - The ID of the product. * `use_recipe` - Whether to use recipe-based stock tracking. # Returns An empty result on success.
signature: pub fn set_product_stock_mode(key: String, id: i32, use_recipe: bool) -> Result<(), String>

name: get_materials
description: Retrieves all material records from the database. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. # Returns A list of all material records.
signature: pub fn get_materials(key: String) -> Result<Vec<Material>, String>

name: create_material
description: Creates a new material record with the specified details. Validates input name and volume, scales the volume to an integer based on precision, and inserts the new material into the database. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `name` - The name of the material. * `type` - The category/type of the material. * `volume` - The total volume of the material (f64). * `quantity` - The initial stock quantity. * `tags` - Optional tags for the material. # Returns The newly created material record.

name: update_material
description: Updates an existing material's information. Performs validation on name and volume, scales the new volume, and updates the record in the database. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `id` - The ID of the material to update. * `name` - The new name for the material. * `type` - The new type for the material. * `volume` - The new total volume for the material. * `quantity` - The new stock quantity. * `tags` - The new tags for the material. # Returns The updated material record.

name: delete_material
description: Deletes a material record by its ID. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `id` - The ID of the material to delete. # Returns The number of deleted records.
signature: pub fn delete_material(key: String, id: i32) -> Result<usize, String>

name: save_image
description: Saves an image from raw byte data to the configured image storage path. Returns the saved Image database record. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `data` - The raw image data as a byte vector. * `filename` - The original filename of the image. # Returns The newly created image record in the database. #[command]
signature: pub fn save_image(key: String, data: Vec<u8>, filename: String) -> Result<database::Image, String>

name: link_product_image
description: Links an existing image to a product. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `product_id` - The ID of the product. * `image_id` - The ID of the image to link. # Returns The newly created product-image association record. #[command]

name: unlink_product_image
description: Unlinks an image from a product. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `product_id` - The ID of the product. * `image_id` - The ID of the image to unlink. # Returns The number of deleted records. #[command]
signature: pub fn unlink_product_image(key: String, product_id: i32, image_id: i32) -> Result<usize, String>

name: clear_product_images
description: Removes all image links from a product. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `product_id` - The ID of the product. # Returns The number of deleted records. #[command]
signature: pub fn clear_product_images(key: String, product_id: i32) -> Result<usize, String>

name: get_product_images
description: Retrieves all images linked to a specific product. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `product_id` - The ID of the product. # Returns A list of images linked to the specified product. #[command]

name: get_all_images
description: Retrieves all images stored in the database. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. # Returns A list of all image records. #[command]

name: delete_image
description: Deletes an image record from the database and removes the associated file from disk. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `image_id` - The ID of the image to delete. # Returns An empty result on success. #[command]
signature: pub fn delete_image(key: String, image_id: i32) -> Result<(), String>

name: get_all_image_links
description: Retrieves all product-to-image associations. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. # Returns A list of all product-image association records. #[command]

name: update_image_position
description: Updates the metadata position of an image. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `image_id` - The ID of the image. * `position` - The new position metadata (e.g., "center", "top"). # Returns The number of updated records. #[command]

name: export_receipts
description: Exports receipts within a specific date range to a file (CSV or XLSX). The export generates a Thai sales tax report format, including VAT calculations based on the configured tax rate. # Arguments * `app` - The Tauri application handle. * `key` - The database encryption key. * `export_path` - The absolute destination file path. * `format` - The export format ("csv" or "xlsx"). * `start_date` - The start of the date range (Unix timestamp in seconds). * `end_date` - The end of the date range (Unix timestamp in seconds). # Returns A success message string.

name: initialize_database
description: Initializes the database using the provided encryption key. Runs all pending database migrations. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. # Returns An empty result on success.
signature: pub fn initialize_database(key: String) -> Result<(), String>

name: check_database_exists
description: Checks if the database file exists on disk. # Arguments * `_app` - The Tauri application handle (unused). # Returns True if the database file exists, false otherwise.
signature: pub fn check_database_exists(_app: tauri::AppHandle) -> Result<bool, String>

name: get_customers
description: Retrieves all customer records from the database. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. # Returns A list of all customer records.
signature: pub fn get_customers(key: String) -> Result<Vec<Customer>, String>

name: create_customer
description: Creates a new customer record. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `name` - The name of the customer. * `tax_id` - Optional tax identification number. * `address` - Optional physical address. * `branch` - Optional branch name or number. # Returns The newly created customer record.

name: update_customer
description: Updates an existing customer record. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `id` - The ID of the customer record to update. * `name` - The new name for the customer. * `tax_id` - Optional new tax identification number. * `address` - Optional new address. * `branch` - Optional new branch information. # Returns The updated customer record.

name: get_categories
description: Retrieves all product categories from the database. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. # Returns A list of product categories.
signature: pub fn get_categories(key: String) -> Result<Vec<Category>, String>

name: create_category
description: Creates a new product category. Validates that the name is not empty, not too long, and unique. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `name` - The name of the new category. # Returns The newly created category.
signature: pub fn create_category(key: String, name: String) -> Result<Category, String>

name: update_category
description: Updates an existing product category's name. Performs validation on name length and ensures uniqueness. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `id` - The ID of the category to update. * `name` - The new name for the category. # Returns The updated category.
signature: pub fn update_category(key: String, id: i32, name: String) -> Result<Category, String>

name: delete_category
description: Deletes a product category by its ID. # Arguments * `key` - The database encryption key. * `id` - The ID of the category to delete. # Returns The number of deleted records.
signature: pub fn delete_category(key: String, id: i32) -> Result<usize, String>

name: run_migrations
description: Embedded database migrations for SQLite. pub const MIGRATIONS: EmbeddedMigrations = embed_migrations!(); Runs all pending database migrations.

name: get_database_path
description: Gets the absolute path to the SQLite database file. Checks for a custom path override in `settings.json` within the app data directory.

name: establish_connection
description: Helper to extract the custom DB storage path from settings.json fn read_custom_db_storage_path(data_dir: &Path) -> Option<PathBuf> { let settings_path = data_dir.join("settings.json"); let content = fs::read_to_string(settings_path).ok()?; let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&content).ok()?; json.get("db_storage_path") .and_then(|v| v.as_str()) .map(PathBuf::from) } Establishes a connection to the encrypted (or soon-to-be encrypted) database.

name: insert_stock
description: Inserts a new stock record into the database and returns the created record.
signature: pub fn insert_stock(conn: &mut SqliteConnection, new_stock: &NewStock) -> Result<Stock, Error>

name: update_stock
description: Updates the quantity of a specific product in stock.

name: remove_stock
description: Deletes a stock record from the database by its ID.
signature: pub fn remove_stock(conn: &mut SqliteConnection, stock_id: i32) -> Result<usize, Error>

name: remove_stock_by_product
description: Deletes a stock record from the database by its associated product ID.
signature: pub fn remove_stock_by_product(conn: &mut SqliteConnection, target_product_id: i32) -> Result<usize, Error>

name: get_stock
description: Retrieves the stock record for a specific product.
signature: pub fn get_stock(conn: &mut SqliteConnection, product_id_target: i32) -> Result<Stock, Error>

name: get_all_stocks
description: Retrieves all stock records from the database.
signature: pub fn get_all_stocks(conn: &mut SqliteConnection) -> Result<Vec<Stock>, Error>

name: sync_product_price_in_stock
description: Synchronizes the product price stored in the stock record.

name: deduct_stock
description: Deducts a specified quantity from the stock of a product.

name: create_recipe_list
description: Creates a new recipe list header for a specific product.

name: get_recipe_list_by_product
description: Retrieves the recipe list header associated with a specific product ID.

name: delete_recipe_list
description: Deletes a recipe list header and all its associated items (via cascading or manual cleanup).

name: add_recipe_item
description: Adds a new ingredient item to an existing recipe list.

name: get_items_by_recipe_list_id
description: Retrieves all ingredient items belonging to a specific recipe list header.

name: update_recipe_item
description: Updates the quantity, unit, and precision of a specific recipe item.

name: remove_recipe_item
description: Removes an individual ingredient item from a recipe list.

name: deduct_stock_from_recipe
description: Deducts material stock based on the recipe of a product sold in a receipt.

name: get_datetime
description: Represents a receipt header, storing high-level transaction information. #[derive(Queryable, Selectable, Serialize, Identifiable, Debug, Clone)] #[diesel(table_name = receipt_list)] #[diesel(primary_key(receipt_id))] pub struct ReceiptList { Unique identifier for the receipt. pub receipt_id: i32, Unix timestamp when the transaction occurred. pub datetime_unix: i64, Optional ID of the customer associated with this transaction. pub customer_id: Option<i32>, } impl ReceiptList { Converts the stored Unix timestamp to a readable `NaiveDateTime`. The `offset_hours` parameter adjusts the timezone (e.g., 7 for ICT/Thailand).
signature: pub fn get_datetime(&self, offset_hours: i32) -> Option<NaiveDateTime>

name: create_receipt_header
description: Creates a new receipt header (transaction record). If `custom_timestamp` is not provided, the current UTC timestamp is used. Returns the newly created `ReceiptList` header.

name: get_all_headers
description: Retrieves all receipt headers from the database.

name: add_item
description: Adds a new transaction item to an existing receipt. This records the product, quantity, and price at the time of sale.

name: add_item_material
description: Adds historical material usage data for a specific receipt item. This is used to track exactly which materials (and what quantities) were used for a product sold via recipe-based stock tracking.

name: get_items_by_header_id
description: Retrieves all items associated with a specific receipt header ID.

name: find_headers_by_date_range
description: Finds receipt headers within a specific Unix timestamp range. Results are ordered by timestamp in descending order (newest first).

name: get_full_receipt
description: Retrieves a full receipt including its header and all associated items. Returns a tuple of `(ReceiptList, Vec<Receipt>)`.

name: link_product_image
description: Creates a link between a product and an image in the database.

name: unlink_product_image
description: Removes a specific link between a product and an image.

name: unlink_all_product_images
description: Removes all image links for a specific product.

name: get_linked_images
description: Retrieves all `Image` records linked to a specific product.

name: get_product_images
description: Retrieves all `ProductImage` link records for a specific product.

name: get_all_links
description: Retrieves all product-image links in the database.

name: insert_product
description: Inserts a new product into the database and returns the created record.
signature: pub fn insert_product(conn: &mut SqliteConnection, newprod: &NewProduct) -> Result<Product, Error>

name: remove_product
description: Deletes a product from the database by its ID.
signature: pub fn remove_product(conn: &mut SqliteConnection, id: i32) -> Result<usize, Error>

name: remove_product_images_link
description: Removes all image associations for a specific product.

name: check_product_dependencies
description: Checks if a product has any dependencies (e.g., used in receipts) that would prevent deletion.

name: find_product_by_title
description: Finds a product by its title. Returns None if not found.

name: get_all_products
description: Retrieves all products from the database.
signature: pub fn get_all_products(conn: &mut SqliteConnection) -> Result<Vec<Product>, Error>

name: update_product
description: Updates an existing product record in the database.
signature: pub fn update_product(conn: &mut SqliteConnection, prod: Product) -> Result<Product, Error>

name: set_product_stock_mode
description: Toggles whether a product should use recipe-based stock tracking.

name: find_product
description: Retrieves a specific product from the database by its ID.
signature: pub fn find_product(conn: &mut SqliteConnection, id: i32) -> Result<Product, Error>

name: get_all_products_with_images
description: Retrieves all products along with their associated image paths.

name: get_all_materials
description: Retrieves all material records from the database.

name: insert_material
description: Inserts a new material record into the database.

name: update_material
description: Updates an existing material record.

name: remove_material
description: Deletes a material record from the database by its ID.
signature: pub fn remove_material(conn: &mut SqliteConnection, target_id: i32) -> QueryResult<usize>

name: find_material
description: Finds a specific material by its ID.
signature: pub fn find_material(conn: &mut SqliteConnection, target_id: i32) -> QueryResult<Material>

name: insert_image
description: Inserts a new image record into the database.

name: get_image
description: Retrieves an image record by its unique ID.

name: get_image_by_hash
description: Retrieves an image record by its file hash (SHA-256). Returns `Ok(None)` if no image with that hash exists.

name: get_all_images
description: Retrieves all image records from the database, ordered by newest first.

name: delete_image
description: Deletes an image record from the database by its ID. Note: This only deletes the database record; the physical file must be handled separately.

name: update_image_path
description: Updates the stored file path of an image.

name: update_image_position
description: Updates the CSS `object-position` value for an image. This is used by the frontend to control which part of the image is visible when it's cropped in a square or fixed-aspect-ratio container.

name: get_all_categories
description: Retrieves all product categories from the database.

name: insert_category
description: Inserts a new category into the database and returns the created record.

name: update_category
description: Updates an existing category record in the database.

name: remove_category
description: Deletes a category from the database by its ID.
signature: pub fn remove_category(conn: &mut SqliteConnection, category_id: i32) -> QueryResult<usize>

name: find_category_by_name
description: Finds a category by its name. Returns None if not found.

name: get_all_customers
description: Retrieves all customers from the database.

name: insert_customer
description: Inserts a new customer record into the database.

name: update_customer_record
description: Updates an existing customer record by their ID. If `update_data.branch` is not provided, it defaults to "00000" (Head Office).

name: export_to_xlsx
description: Exports an `ExportTable` to an XLSX file. # Arguments * `table` - The table to export. * `path` - The destination file path.
signature: pub fn export_to_xlsx<P: AsRef<Path>>(table: &ExportTable, path: P) -> Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>

name: export_to_xlsx_sheets
description: Exports multiple named sheets into a single XLSX workbook. # Arguments * `sheets` - A slice of tuples containing the sheet name and the table reference. * `path` - The destination file path.

name: import_from_xlsx
description: Imports an `ExportTable` from the first sheet of an XLSX file. # Arguments * `path` - The source file path. # Returns An `ExportTable` containing the data from the XLSX file.
signature: pub fn import_from_xlsx<P: AsRef<Path>>(path: P) -> Result<ExportTable, Box<dyn Error>>

name: build_thai_sales_tax_report
description: Represents a single row in a Thai Sales Tax Report. #[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)] pub struct TaxReportRow { Date of the transaction (typically formatted as dd/mm/yyyy). pub date: String, The invoice or receipt number. pub invoice_no: String, The name of the customer. pub customer_name: String, The customer's Tax ID (13 digits). pub tax_id: String, The branch description (e.g., "Head Office" or branch number). pub branch_address: String, The taxable amount before VAT. pub amount_before_vat: f64, The calculated VAT amount. pub vat_amount: f64, The total amount including VAT. pub grand_total: f64, } Builds an `ExportTable` formatted as a Thai Sales Tax Report. This function translates the `TaxReportRow` data into a localized table with Thai headers as required by accounting standards. # Arguments * `rows` - A vector of `TaxReportRow` data to include in the report. # Returns An `ExportTable` ready for export to CSV or XLSX.

name: export_to_ods
description: Exports an `ExportTable` to an ODS (OpenDocument Spreadsheet) file. # Arguments * `table` - The table to export. * `path` - The destination file path.
signature: pub fn export_to_ods<P: AsRef<Path>>(table: &ExportTable, path: P) -> Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>

name: import_from_ods
description: Imports an `ExportTable` from the first sheet of an ODS file. # Arguments * `path` - The source file path. # Returns An `ExportTable` containing the data from the ODS file.
signature: pub fn import_from_ods<P: AsRef<Path>>(path: P) -> Result<ExportTable, Box<dyn Error>>

name: sanitize_cell_text
description: Represents a value in a spreadsheet cell. Supports basic types like text, numbers, booleans, and empty cells. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)] #[serde(untagged)] pub enum CellValue { A text string. Text(String), A floating-point number. Number(f64), An integer number. Int(i64), A boolean value. Bool(bool), An empty cell. None, } impl std::fmt::Display for CellValue { fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result { match self { CellValue::Text(s) => write!(f, "{}", s), CellValue::Number(n) => write!(f, "{}", n), CellValue::Int(n) => write!(f, "{}", n), CellValue::Bool(b) => write!(f, "{}", b), CellValue::None => write!(f, ""), } } } Sanitizes a string to prevent Formula Injection (CSV/XLSX/ODS). Prepends a single quote if the string starts with characters that could trigger formula execution in spreadsheet applications (=, +, -, @). # Arguments * `s` - The string to sanitize. # Returns The sanitized string.

name: new
description: A generic representation of a spreadsheet table. Contains headers and rows of `CellValue`s, providing methods to export to different formats (CSV, XLSX, ODS). #[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)] pub struct ExportTable { The table headers. pub headers: Vec<String>, The table rows. pub rows: Vec<Vec<CellValue>>, } impl ExportTable { Creates a new `ExportTable` with the specified headers.

name: add_row
description: Adds a row of values to the table.
signature: pub fn add_row(&mut self, row: Vec<CellValue>)

name: edit_cell
description: Edits a specific cell in the table. # Returns `true` if the cell was successfully updated, `false` otherwise.
signature: pub fn edit_cell(&mut self, row_idx: usize, col_idx: usize, value: CellValue) -> bool

name: export_csv
description: Exports the table to a CSV file.
signature: pub fn export_csv<P: AsRef<Path>>(&self, path: P) -> Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>

name: export_xlsx
description: Exports the table to an XLSX file.
signature: pub fn export_xlsx<P: AsRef<Path>>(&self, path: P) -> Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>

name: export_ods
description: Exports the table to an ODS file.
signature: pub fn export_ods<P: AsRef<Path>>(&self, path: P) -> Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>

name: import_csv
description: Imports an `ExportTable` from a CSV file.
signature: pub fn import_csv<P: AsRef<Path>>(path: P) -> Result<Self, Box<dyn Error>>

name: import_xlsx
description: Imports an `ExportTable` from an XLSX file.
signature: pub fn import_xlsx<P: AsRef<Path>>(path: P) -> Result<Self, Box<dyn Error>>

name: import_ods
description: Imports an `ExportTable` from an ODS file.
signature: pub fn import_ods<P: AsRef<Path>>(path: P) -> Result<Self, Box<dyn Error>>

name: export_xlsx_sheets
description: Export multiple named `ExportTable`s as separate sheets in one XLSX file. # Arguments * `sheets` - A slice of tuples containing the sheet name and the table reference. * `path` - The destination file path.

name: export_to_csv
description: Exports an `ExportTable` to a CSV file. # Arguments * `table` - The table to export. * `path` - The destination file path.
signature: pub fn export_to_csv<P: AsRef<Path>>(table: &ExportTable, path: P) -> Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>

name: import_from_csv
description: Imports an `ExportTable` from a CSV file. # Arguments * `path` - The source file path. # Returns An `ExportTable` containing the data from the CSV file.
signature: pub fn import_from_csv<P: AsRef<Path>>(path: P) -> Result<ExportTable, Box<dyn Error>>

name: save_image
description: Errors that can occur during image processing and storage operations. #[derive(Error, Debug)] pub enum ImageError { Errors originating from the database layer. #[error("Database error: {0}")] Database(#[from] diesel::result::Error), Errors originating from standard I/O operations. #[error("IO error: {0}")] Io(#[from] std::io::Error), Errors originating from the image processing library. #[error("Image error: {0}")] Image(#[from] image::ImageError), Connection-related errors, typically stringified database errors. #[error("Connection error: {0}")] Connection(String), The target image directory could not be determined or found. #[error("Directory not found")] DirectoryNotFound, The file extension is not supported or the filename is invalid. #[error("Invalid file extension: {0}")] InvalidExtension(String), } const ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS: &[&str] = &["png", "jpg", "jpeg", "webp"]; Saves an image to disk and creates a record in the database. This function handles hash-based deduplication, path traversal protection, and atomic file writes. # Arguments * `data` - The raw image byte data. * `filename` - The original filename of the image. * `target_dir` - Optional custom directory to save the image. Defaults to `images/` next to the DB. * `key` - The database encryption key. # Returns The created or existing `Image` record.

name: verify_image
description: Verifies that an image file exists and matches its stored hash. # Arguments * `image_id` - The ID of the image to verify. * `key` - The database encryption key. # Returns `true` if the image is valid and matches the hash, `false` otherwise.
signature: pub fn verify_image(image_id: i32, key: &str) -> Result<bool, ImageError>

name: delete_image_file
description: Deletes an image file from disk. # Arguments * `file_path` - The absolute path to the file to delete.
